task_completion.description: The YDBGui web interface is open and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser window displaying the YDBGui interface.

task_completion.description: The ^SC global (Hospital Location file) is navigated to and opened in the YDBGui interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The YDBGui interface shows the ^SC global selected or being viewed in its database explorer or viewer.

task_completion.description: At least 5 different entries in the ^SC global are visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A list, tree view, or multiple data nodes showing at least 5 distinct hospital location entries under the ^SC global.

task_completion.description: Both a CLINIC (Type: C) and a WARD (Type: W) are visible, including their names and types.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The database entries clearly display the names of the locations and their corresponding types, specifically showing at least one 'C' (Clinic) and one 'W' (Ward).

integrity.description: The agent used the YDBGui web interface rather than terminal commands to view the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The hospital location data is displayed within the YDBGui web interface, not in a terminal, SSH session, or command-line interface.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual YDBGui application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the YDBGui interface with its standard UI elements (menus, trees, tables), rather than a text editor, static document, or mocked UI.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type out the expected answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The types 'C' and 'W' and the 5 entries are shown as genuine database output rendered by the YDBGui application, not typed into a notepad, terminal echo, or search bar.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target interface is the YDBGui web interface. The target VistA global for Hospital Locations (File 44) is ^SC. The task requires viewing at least 5 entries in the ^SC global, specifically ensuring that at least one Clinic (Type 'C') and one Ward (Type 'W') are visible with their names and types.
